sectionOrderRules: Hero must include company type or trade focus, service area if known, and a primary CTA to request a quote.
Service summary must mention actual work categories relevant to the business, not vague promises.
Trust proof should highlight real qualifications, industry rules, guarantees or work methods only when supported by the business data.
Gallery or testimonials may appear before trust-proof when project material exists.
Final contact-cta must appear near the bottom and should not repeat the exact same copy as the hero.
Services intro must explain what kinds of specialized construction or installation work the company performs.
Service list items must be concrete, for example renovation, tiling, painting, groundworks, electrical or plumbing work when applicable.
If pricing-overview is shown, it must describe pricing structure honestly and avoid fixed promises unless supplied by the business.
A contact CTA should follow the service details to capture quote intent.
Portfolio intro must explain that the page shows examples of completed or ongoing work.
Project gallery should prioritize authentic project photos, especially before-and-after material when available.
Project highlights must describe the type of job, scope or outcome without inventing customer names or project facts.
Contact CTA should invite similar project inquiries after the showcased work.
Contact info must include real phone, email and address details when available.
Contact-form-section should be oriented around quote requests and allow the visitor to describe the job.
Map may be shown when the business has a physical address or office worth displaying.
Opening hours should only be shown when the business has defined contact or office hours.
